transcript.text: Hi, how's it going?
I'm good, Hario.
Good.
It's Charles.
know it just says Polaroid.
That's my meeting room here in New York.
This meeting is being recorded.
Are you all going to be joining from one room today?
No, I am in New York and Peter and Jess and Brianna are all in San Francisco, so they may all be together.
Okay, okay, got it.
Hi, everyone.
Hi, Aida, how are you?
How's it going?
Good.
How are you?
I am good.
Busy, busy time of year, I'm sure.
Yeah, it is, and it doesn't help that it gets dark at 4 p.m., I'm still taking vitamin D supplements this week in the hopes that, like, my energy levels...
We'll kind of like get to a better place because this is truly brutal.
I hadn't even thought about that, but maybe that's why my energy level is low, For me, I always forget.
then at some point in January, I get really bad, like anxiety and I just feel horrible all the time.
And then I realized, oh, God, I need like vitamin D supplements.
And then after three days, I'm like back to normal.
So it's just a lack of sunlight.
All right, cool.
I think we have everybody here right now.
So I'm going to go ahead and share the agenda for today.
Before we begin, I wanted to ask if there's anything top of mind before we get into the updates.
These seem right to me.
We did have one of our reviewers, James, for Jess and Charles' sake, PMMs.
He had a question about the review process.

Or maybe it's like feedback on the review process, which we don't need to bleed with, but maybe just jam on that and see if there's a better way or better like checkpoint for him to come in, especially on like positioning storytelling.

Like the high level was that he was like, oh, I think like before they generated this whole thing, I could have given them a quick, like, here's how I would kind of like storytell based on this topic.
Like, and I'd be curious to get your perspective on the growthx side of like, is that a helpful extra step if there's a better way to do that?
If it's just a matter of like, hey, let's sit and update the artifacts, happy to do that as well.
But just wanted to voice that feedback and see what we can do.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think there are two ways to go about this depending on what sort of the end goal is.
So if the end goal is to provide like a.
Advice on or like guidance around how to talk about a specific topic and like how to weave Hex in or like what are the things that we should highlight.
That is something that should be brought up before we start creating the content.
And in that case, the best way to provide that feedback would be by leaving comments in Airtable.
We have a batch of content that's approved to start.
And I think.
Hold on, let me just find it like the status really quickly.
So we have like 22 pieces that are approved to start.
So I think that might be like a manageable number for like the PM to just go through and kind of see what's interesting, what stands out.
And then they can leave feedback just by like, like leaving a comment here and just like providing suggestions on how.
We could target it.

If there is feedback on the way that we talk about Hex in the content, like what are the benefits that we're highlighting, like what's the story that we're presenting, that type of feedback would best be incorporated into the product services artifact.

And in that case, it could be, we could do a meeting, we don't have to do a meeting, they can also like record a loom, or just like an audio, like just kind of talking through what are like the things that they've noticed, but it's like a high priority for us to kind of talk about.
And then we would update the product artifact with that information.
The goal is really for the PMs to not have a lot of, like, just in terms of the review, we really don't want people to be reviewing this on an ongoing.
Like our goal is really to kind of ensure that like there's maybe one reviewer and then after that it's been like it's good to publish, you know.
Well, that makes sense.
I'll take that to him and it sounds like it falls more into the first category.
So you can see if I'm getting him a preview into maybe like the next month's worth of approved articles and let him leave feedback in the Airtable.
Yeah, yeah, that's totally fine.
You can just give me his email and then I can just send him an invite for the Airtable.
Awesome.
Thank you.
All right.
And then for the content that we've delivered so far, so four articles have been published, which is great.
I'm really excited.
And then I think in the next couple of days, they should be indexed.
If they haven't been indexed already, I haven't had the chance to check here.
Cool.
Cool.

Google Search Console account, and then next week we should start seeing like impressions come in as the content gets ranked in Google Search, and then for the articles that are currently being reviewed, so there are five that are with the Hex team, these are, it's this five, let us know if we should update the status, but I do believe that these are all with the team for review.
Aida, I just pretty much finished reviewing all of these, except for the Jupyter one, so I think they're done.
Pete, do you know if James is done reviewing these?
I really just asked him to look at the tab lovers, Power BI, and Jupyter, and he did review and leave feedback for those.
Okay, so Aida, think you could put done on all of them except for the Jupyter, I will still, I will do that after this call.
Okay, cool, perfect.
Yeah, I think in that case, we can like implement the feedback tomorrow and then stage them as well.
Okay, great.
And then these are the five that we're working on this week.
They will be ready by the end of day tomorrow.
So I'm going to share them as well.
And we did get the, when did I say customer study, case study?
We already did implement the Figma case study into our artifacts.
So I wanted to show you what Kavishka, who's our content manager, what he did to, to kind of automatically like pull these case studies into the article drafts.
So this is your, this is your workspace in Atlas, which is our content generation platform.
And we have like different artifacts here.
You review the company context, the audience personas, and the writing guidelines.
We also also have this.
Unrelated to the case studies, we also have examples of good, which are articles that capture your tone and are good examples of the kind of content that we want to emulate.
So these are the ones where like that we can change these like like I think we've already updated this like four times.

But whenever, you know, we, if you notice that like the tone is a little bit off or you want to adjust it, or if you have like an even better article that you want us to like reference, just let us know and then we can like drop it in here.
And it's going to like this artifact gets called at the drafting stage so that the content emulates the voice and the tone in this artifact.
Nice.
Aida, is it, can we put more than two articles or like more than one article in here?
Would you just recommend one?
I think like one or I would.
I to use, like, I wouldn't do more than one because this one is quite long.
So it's like we have the token length here and this is kind of the amount of information that's required for the AI to, like, process this artifact.
So what we can do is we can provide, like, one half of an article and then one half of another article.
Like, so it's not, like, the whole thing, but, like, the intro and maybe, like, an H2 section.
Okay, I'll think about that.
I only ask because I really liked the way the Embedded Analytics article read and I liked the way it was structured.
There were certain things about that that I think are just, like, slightly different than some pieces of this.
So, yeah, I'll revisit that and highlight what I think makes it strong outside of this article.
Yeah, that's, that's.
It's great.
I think we can do like a combination.
could like use what is natural language BI and then we can also incorporate the embedded analytics one.
But we can also, we have like a test pipeline for articles set up separately.
So we can like test two different versions of like good examples and then like figure out which one is best.
So, and, and if this one like, and if we figure out like, okay, we just like completely screwed everything up.
We need to like go back.
We can always go back.
Like it's, we just like revert to an earlier version of the artifact.
So it's not like a big deal.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
Let me revisit that one and leave some specific comments and then maybe we can make a decision async.
Cool.
Yeah, that works.
But to go back to the case studies.
So we have a custom artifact here that's called customer spotlights and it includes.
It's different case studies that we want to reference in the content.
So we've added Figma here as well.
It's a condensed version of the case study, but it's called at the drafting stage and then the AI inserts them like at the most appropriate, in the most appropriate section.
So that way, you know, we don't have to do this manually.

And, you know, there's, I think there's always a chance when a human is like manually adding case studies, there's always a chance that like we might forget something or there's like a piece of like content that, context that we end up missing.
So this is an easier way for us to insert these into the content.
Does this have all of our case studies, Aida?
I believe so, because it has Doximity, Greenhouse, anything that's on your website is in here, I think.
I wonder...
I'm curious about your opinion on this and maybe some people on the team.
Like, do we want to, y'all, remove maybe Infinite Lambda or, I don't know, maybe Alge Trails, maybe some of these companies that are, like, very unknown?
But I guess that the benefit to me is that it's still proving a use case through some real company.
It's just a company that a lot of people might not recognize.
So, how do y'all feel about that?

My gut reaction is that we're going to have, to my understanding, three or four, I was going say , maybe that's not the right word, but three or four juicy customer spotlights coming in the next few weeks or months.
So maybe we rotate those in and just hold tight for those few weeks.
Yeah, I guess it doesn't hurt us.
That's...
It's like, well, if somebody sees us working with some random company, they don't know.
Like, that doesn't really, I don't think that really affects our reputation.
And also, a lot of those articles already layer in, like, a bigger name as well, like Notion or Pigma.
So, okay.
All right.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think we can definitely, like, prioritize the bigger names at the assignment direction stage.
But, yeah, I think it doesn't hurt, honestly.
Like, just linking to other case studies is always helpful.
All right.
I think, not sure if there's anything.
Oh, yeah.
And then we had another update.
And this is just, like, more to give you an idea of what we've been doing on the back end.
So, my understanding is that, you know, you had, Jessica, had some feedback just around, like, the tone and how, like...

Conversational it was and like just making it a bit more human and like not stiff and we did some experiments and it turned out that if we instructed the AI in the writing guidelines to kind of use like a warmer, like more human tone, it would affect the content in a way that like it might, it would distill information to make it easier to understand but then important context would be lost and it would affect the quality of the content because we would be kind of like glossing over some details that are actually really important.

So what we did was we created like two separate writing guideline artifacts so the writing guidelines, the ones that you reviewed are that's this artifact, but then we also have the writing style guide, which is called at the very end.
And it just is applied after the draft has already been generated.

And it just kind of like finds sentences where sentences that would benefit from like a more human writing style without like doing like a heavier rewrite of the content and like impacting the actual information that's presented.
It's weird, but we were like experimenting a little bit and we found that it was actually better when we separated these artifacts rather than like trying to do everything all in one.
So that's what we're doing right now, just to make like just to give you like an idea of what we've done to improve the output.
That's so fascinating.
Thank you for sharing that, Aida.
That's, yeah.
Yeah, that is, I mean, we learn something new every week with AI.
It's like with, and it's, the thing is that, you know, with different clients, like different use cases emerge and, you know, you are, Hex is unique in the sense that you.
You.
Like, the subject matter is complex, but the tone isn't trying to be.
So we have to kind of be creative in how we approach this, like, process.

And we found that by, like, chunking or, like, separating these different artifacts, we get better results rather than trying to just, like, insert everything into, like, one giant artifact that doesn't really, like, the AI just gets confused and doesn't know what it's supposed to do.
That's really cool.
And maybe a tip that we can also borrow here internally.
So thank you for sharing.
Don't worry.
So it's been something that we've kind of learned by doing.
It's taken us a little bit.
But, yeah, it's good to kind of, like, crack the code finally.
It feels really good.
So, yeah.
All right.
And then let me see.
Yeah.
So for the next step, so we're going to stage and publish the additional articles.
I believe that
We are good to publish.
Like, I think, have you published the blog post that you wanted to get on your website today?
Oh, okay.
That's moving to Monday.
So anyway, but yes, we, yes.
So you guys are, yeah, green light to come back and to upload or stage everything in Contentful.
Okay.
But thank you all for holding off on that.
Sometimes our Contentful is finicky and there's just been a lot of moving deadlines here.
Okay.
That makes sense.
All right.
So yeah, when we get the green light on these, we're going to go and stage them.
So you're going to publish them or do you want us to?
Okay.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
We'll publish.
Okay.
All right.
Cool.

And then I owe you one more thing by the end of this week, which is a summary of everything we've done in phase one and like what the plans are for phase two and like how we're going to content production and how we want to approach content prioritization and we're also going.
In the next couple of weeks, we'll send you a technical audit of your site.
So just to highlight any potential technical issues that could be impacting SEO and search rankings, that's something that Talal, who's our SEO person, is working on right now.
That sounds good.
Aida, one quick question I had is, like, for self-serve analytics, I know we already have an SEO article for this, but I don't, it's probably not ranking.
And anyway, I, like, I saw another version of that with embedded analytics.
Like, y'all built one, and I, like, quickly typed in embedded analytics that we had, and it wasn't ranking, so I just unpublished it.
Um, that's what I, I mean, I think I'm okay with that course moving forward.
I, I just wanted to double check that it does seem like.
Like there's a few duplications of topics that we've written about, but yeah, just clearly have so little clout that they're not showing up.
In which case, maybe we do just sunset, unpublish those and roll with y'all's new version.
I think in that case, it really, yeah, it depends on like the SEO performance.
Guys, I'll check with the team because we typically do always do like a Google search before we start generating the content.
We just like enter your domain name and then the primary keyword to find what else is on your website.
So I'll check in to see if something might have slipped through.

What we want to do is if you already have an existing piece of content on your site, we would rather like rewrite it completely and just like update the existing URL with just like all brand new content.
want you want
Or if the article never really had any traffic and was just not ranking anywhere, I mean, you can remove it and then just redirect to the new blog post.
That also works.
But I'm going to just let them know to just be sure to double check for every keyword that we're writing.
Because you do have content on your site and we have, like, sometimes we might want to refresh or rewrite something rather than just publishing a brand new URL.
Yeah.
I can also come back in here, like, a few things that might be difficult.

One, I think if you search for some of those keywords within our blog search bar, they won't come up because we've elected to hide the previous SEO content from the blog, from showing up in search results on the blog.
And what was the other thing I was going to say?
Yeah.
But anyway, I mean, it does seem telling that, like, if y'all are doing Google searches with the domain, with the key.
And it's just not showing up at all.
I don't know.
Yeah.
So anyway, I can come in and I'll drop some in our Notion content calendar.
because like, I'm very familiar with the recent SEO push that we did, I'll go back and link what I know through those domains and put them in here.
Okay.
All right.
All right.
Cool.
I'll let the team know then just to make sure, because we typically do use just like a Google search.
So even if something's hidden on your site, we could still find it as long as it's not like the indexed, which can also sometimes happen, but it's not as common.
Okay.
All right.
I think those are all the updates that I had.
Do you guys have any other questions?
None on my end.
I would love, I don't know if next week makes sense, or I guess there is no week after.
Okay.
All right.
There's a week after.
We won't be together, though.
I would love a walkthrough of the reports and reporting that we can expect just to familiarize this group with what we're looking at from a metric standpoint.
So it sounds like either next week or if newly in the new year, just to help orient this group.
Yeah, so in terms of what we use for reporting, this was like a few weeks ago.
So I think it was like in the beginning of the engagement.
We did a walkthrough of Scrunch, and then we also have a dashboard in Looker with the GSC and GA4 data.
So those are the two main platforms that we use to track performance.
But I think I can record a loom, and then you can kind of share it internally.
And can show you what, like, all the different pages are, because the Looker report is, like, multi-page.
Sweet.
That would be awesome.
Yeah.
I mean, in terms of, like, what we track initially, like, for example, for the four that just got published, we're initially just looking, okay, did this get indexed quickly?
If it did, okay, where is it ranking?
Is it getting impressions?
Is it getting clicks?
Like, those are, like, the first signals.
Signals.
then, as we ramp up, we also look at referral traffic from LLMs to these blog posts.
And then, anything that you are using internally to track leads, like, getting access to that data is also helpful.
I don't know what, like, how you're measuring content internally, but that would also be helpful to know.
Yeah, this is probably a broader discussion we need to have.
We don't have a great way, right now, to have, like, page-level attribution, as far as I know.
If...
For either signups or gated content downloads.
Okay.
Which, I don't know if that's possible to cover in your technical analysis or what a team can look at, but that has been a struggle for us historically.
Are you using any, what are you using internally to measure site performance user?
We use like Rudderstack for web events and then that pushes through to our warehouse and then into Fx.
Okay.
All right.
I got it.
We've made a few different attempts at cleaning up our GA4.
The latest dead end we hit was that we use like a bot tool for like smoke testing our website and product.
And we don't pay for a tier high enough to get like specific IPs that we could exclude.
So we have like a ton of inflated, like recorded sessions.
Which makes everything else.
So.
It's nonsensical.
And they all go under undefined referral buckets.
Okay.
Okay.
Got it.
All right.
I think, yeah, tracking that type of data is, I don't know.
I mean, it depends really.
We have some clients that track just the performance of our blogs.
Like, they add, like, UTM codes to, like, these links right here and right here so that they can kind of see, okay, like, did any traffic come, like, any, like, leads click through from our blogs.
But, yeah, I think I can record those walkthroughs and share them in Loom.
And, yeah, you can let me know if you have any questions or if you need us to, like, customize the reports in any way.
Okay.
Cool.
Anything else?
Well, we could set up a UTM and maybe we do do that or low-hanging fruit, at least.
And Aida, can give it to your team for, like, request MO and sign up for trial?
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Yeah.

And I think with, I know we're almost at time, but because you're using Contentful, I would, like, if, it would be cool if we had, like, CTA blocks, like, kind of like this one, but that we could, like, CTA blocks that, like, go to the enterprise page or, like, different, different solutions, like, team pages or, like, all these different, like, product pages.
That way, when we're talking about, for example, embedded analytics, we can, like, add a CTA block that kind of gets attention.
Because, like, a CTA is always going to be, like, more eye-grabbing rather than just, like, a link.
So, and I know that Contentful does allow you to set this up pretty easily, unless I'm wrong, but it should be, like, more friendly.
this

like built exploration reports and we did like a really big push by like adding different CTAs to all the content that we were publishing.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress, refine the review process, and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Review Process:** PMMs will now provide high-level storytelling feedback on topics in Airtable *before* content is drafted, preventing late-stage rewrites.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** 4 articles are live, 5 are approved for staging, and 5 more will be delivered this week. Staging is approved for Monday.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
  - [**AI Workflow Refined:** The AI now uses two separate artifacts—one for technical content, one for tone—to generate human-sounding drafts without sacrificing accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=823.0)
  - [**Performance Tracking:** A Loom walkthrough of the Looker/Scrunch dashboards is due next week. Hex will investigate creating UTMs and inline CTAs to enable page-level lead attribution.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1313.0)

## Topics

### Content Review & Pipeline

  - [**Problem:** PMM James requested earlier input on content topics to guide the narrative before drafts are generated.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [**Solution:** A two-part review process was established:](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
      - [**Topic Feedback:** PMMs will comment in Airtable on approved topics (22 currently) to guide storytelling *before* drafting.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
      - [**Strategic Feedback:** For high-level messaging, PMMs can update the "product services artifact" via Loom or a meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=241.0)
  - [**Pipeline Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [**Live:** 4 articles](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [**Approved for Staging:** 5 articles (all feedback implemented except for one final review by Jessica)](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
      - [**In Progress:** 5 articles, due EOD Friday](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)
  - [**Staging:** Approved for Monday, as Hex's internal blog post launch was moved.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)

### AI Workflow & Artifacts

  - [**Tone & Accuracy Challenge:** Initial attempts to make the AI tone more "human" sacrificed technical accuracy by oversimplifying concepts.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=830.0)
  - [**Solution:** The workflow was split into two artifacts:](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=823.0)
    1.  [**Writing Guidelines:** Focuses solely on technical content accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=883.0)
    2.  [**Writing Style Guide:** Applied *after* drafting to refine tone without altering facts.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=883.0)
    <!-- end list -->
      - [**Result:** This separation successfully generates human-sounding drafts without losing critical context.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=823.0)
  - [**"Examples of Good" Artifact:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**Function:** Guides the AI's structure and voice.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**Constraint:** Limited to \~1 article due to token length.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
      - [**Action:** Jessica will identify specific elements from the "Embedded Analytics" article to combine with the current example.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=576.0)
  - [**Automated Case Study Insertion:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
      - [**Function:** A "customer spotlights" artifact automatically inserts condensed case studies (e.g., Figma) into relevant sections.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)
      - [**Decision:** Keep all case studies, as the benefit of proving a use case outweighs the risk of using lesser-known companies.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)

### Performance Tracking & Attribution

  - [**Challenge:** Hex lacks page-level attribution for signups and gated downloads.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1429.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** GA4 data is inflated by unfilterable bot traffic, making it unreliable.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1471.0)
  - [**Solution Paths:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1554.0)
      - [**UTMs:** Add UTMs to CTAs (e.g., "Request Demo") to track blog-sourced leads.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1554.0)
      - [**Inline CTAs:** Build reusable Contentful blocks for prominent CTAs, which are proven to drive higher engagement than text links.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)
  - [**Reporting Walkthrough:** Aida will record a Loom video explaining the Scrunch and Looker dashboards.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)
  - [**Duplicate Content:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1102.0)
      - [**Problem:** GrowthX is drafting new articles for topics where Hex has existing, non-ranking content.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1102.0)
      - [**Action:** Jessica will identify these URLs in Notion. GrowthX will then either rewrite the existing post or publish a new one with a redirect.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1102.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jessica:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)

      - [List URLs of existing, non-ranking content in Notion.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1264.0)
  - [**Peter:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)
      - [Confirm with the design team if inline CTA blocks exist in Contentful.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)
  - [**GrowthX Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)
      - [Stage the 5 approved articles in Contentful for Monday publication.](https://fathom.video/share/s5d2Xt7Gi3EtBUkvwUmJ54QsDs-shcLB?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)
